transcript.text:
Man, I'm starving
Are you ready?
I'll go with Hanwoo (Korean Beef) today!
[I'm going to grab whatever looks tasty]
(Oh my)
This is sick
[15 different cuts of Hanwoo beef mukbang]
[Trying all different types of beef]
[Let's go pig out today]
You are incredible
Thank you hahaha
Hello everyone~~
I am here at Gyeongju today
[Cheomseongdae, an astronomical observatory constructed in the Kingdom of Silla]
[Dearungwon, a cluster of 23 tumuli is located in the belt]
As you know, Gyeongju is one of the most popular tourist destinations

I heard Gyeongju is also known for Hanwoo
So here I am
Let's go!
Oh wow, so this place is a butcher shop with a restaurant inside.
[Ready to rob this place?]
I'm super excited
Tenderloin
Neck Chain
Center Heel? This one looks delicious
Wow, this is a lot
This is ridiculous
I've never had this many types of beef before
Oh well, Bon Appetit!
I can't wait
I will start with something not too greasy
This one doesn't look greasy at all

Let's go with anchangsal (outside skirt)
[Anchangsal (outside skirt)]
Looks yummy!
I'm going to put all of it
It's a lot of meat
Oh! it's already cooked
That was fast
I will take 'em out
Beef should never be over cooked
Looks so delicious!
Bon Appetit!
Gotta dip it in salt for the first bite
It has a really rich and strong flavor of beef
It's super savory too
I love its robust texture along with the unique flavor
It's also extremely savory and meaty

Put a little wasabi
Is it too little?
It's super yummy
[Picking the next one]
This one is called eobjinsal (short beef plate) I've never tried it before
It cooks so fast
For real
It takes only 3 sec
for each side
Let me try the rare one
It's so chewy
I'm pretty sure almost everyone of you have been to Gyeongju for a school trip.
Our generation had no choice. We kinda had to go and stop by Bulguk Temple and stuff
I didn't know Gyeongju was known for beef tho
2 pieces
Dip it in ssamjang (Korean spicy dipping sauce)

Not so good
Wasabi for the last bite
Beef can't go wrong with wasabi
Buchaesal! (Top Blade)
[Buchaesal (Top Blade)]
It's like comb-cut sliced pork belly
No more space on the grill
But how does this get cooked so fast?
It's already cooked
Oh well, I need to try rare, medium, and everything
Let me take the whole thing
Huh?
What the
I didn't expect a buchaesal to be this tasty to be honest.
2 more pieces
Dip it in salt

I've never had such a high quality buchaesal in my life
It's amazing
Wow
A medium rare is chewy and tender at the same time, but the medium well is more chewy
I like both
Again, wasabi for the last bite
I put too much
The next one is salchisal (chuck tail flap)
This one is my favorite, so I bought two
It looks incredible!
Gotta take 'em out fast
and cook them more later
You know, it tastes poor if overcooked
Salchi!
The best one
Look, it's tender and wobbly like pudding

Let's do it
Dip it in salt for the first bite!
This is unreal
It literally disappeared after a couple of chews. Super chewy and juicy, it melted in my mouth.
Wrap it with myeonginamul (wild leek)
They should make a good combination because salchisal is a bit greasy
I told you
It catches the greasiness
Since I just had the greasy part, let's go with something less greasy
I love how I can balance it out
I'll go with hanging tender
A rare piece
Where are my medium-rare
Right here
and that's the medium
Medium well done

Argh.. I don't like it well done
Wow this is truly rare
I like its texture. Incredible
Let me try the medium well done
I used to like greasy parts way more than the non-greasy cuts.
But now they both taste super delicious. I think my taste has changed
This texture tho
It's hard to describe
Wasabi on top
Wasabi actually tastes better with the greasy parts
Salt is the best for hanging tender because the meat itself is super tasty.
Can you see this fine grain? Its texture heavily depends on the grain
It's chewy.. and...
sigh.. It's hard to describe
The next one is..
Saewoosal (Ribeye Cap)! It's actually one of the most oily cuts

It was named after its arched appearance
I can make a heart
I will call this a heart meat instead of saewoosal from now on.
It looks fantastic
It tastes better when cooked a little longer
Salt
It's super tender
How can it be this juicy?
It's extremely juicy
Next one is ansim (tenderloin)!
I need to cook it longer for steaks
This is pretty much a premium beef ansim steak!
I am not sure if it's true but I heard this cut is used for beef yukhoe (tartare) as well
I think it's okay to eat it raw
This should be fine. Let me try it
with a little bit of salt

I see why people eat it raw
It's super tender and delicious
It's way more tender than yukhoe
Hello, sir!
Hello!
Oh, that's doenjang jjigae (soybean paste soup)!
Why don't you just eat an entire cow?
Though we, millennium Hanwoo, are not well known yet, we've been continuously improving
We will try our best to serve you today!
Thank you very much! My pleasure
So juicy
I like it more with wasabi
Dropped
doesn't matter
Wrap it
With myeonginamul

Last piece!
I'm curious about this jjigae. Look at the color of it
I can tell it's good just by looking at the color of it.
I love it
Let's go with a deungsim (sirloin) this time
So this part is the deungsim and the outer meat is the saewoosal (ribeye cap)
Wow, it cooks fast
I'mma take it out
Amazing
That was the saewoosal
I can tell with its texture
This looks like a sirloin
Very delicious and tender
The next one is thin flank
I don't get full eating beef
The taste of think flank heavily depends on its quality.

Let's see
I'll eat it now
with a little bit of salt
This is the most chewy one so far
Dip it in ssamjang
I can't even talk because of all the juice in it
I can't wait for the next round
It is a long-awaited ggotsal (bottom sirloin)!
I didn't know the full name of ggotsal was deungsim ggotsal.
I fell in love with it when I first had it. This is truly a taste of heaven
[I don't have time to wait!]
Looks gorgeous
This tastes very similar to salchisal. I heard they are almost the same thing
Maybe I'm wrong
But I like ggotsal better
This is the best

Wrap it with myeonginamul
Last piece
Super tasty
I want something not too greasy this time
[Arongsatae (Hind shank)]
This looks like a pork tendon from suyuk (boiled meat) restaurants.
You know, like a gelatinous texture
I've never had a grilled arongsatae
Arongsatae is commonly used for suyuk only
so I've never had a grilled one
Dip it in salt
This shouldn't be used only for suyuk (boiled meat)
So good
I love its chewy and gelatinous texture
Gotta dip it in the sauce
Wow it's really good

Let's continue
I'll do galbisal (rib finger) this time
It looks delicious
I think ggotgalbi, ggotsal, and salchisal taste pretty much the same
Hello
Are you almost done already? Yes haha
Thanks for the meat.
Did you like it? This is the best of the best
I really liked it
I've never seen a woman eating this much in my 30-year career.
HAHAHA, really?
Please enjoy the rest. Thank you
So they are slightly different
Salchisal is more tender, and galbisal has a tougher texture
I love its tough texture
This time

I'll go for more arongsatae!
Nvm, I'm tripping
It's jebichuri (neck chain)
I'm just gonna wing it
Jebichuri is one of the few special cuts
I've never had this many types of beef before
Let's do it
I'll move them over and put the rest of salchisal
Salchisal!
Salchisal, again, is pretty greasy, but jebichuri is not at all
I will try both at the same time
How was your meal? I still have this one left
This cut is for yukhoe (beef tartare) but it's not chopped. I will make a to-go for you
Can I just eat it like that
Nah, I'll make it to go for you
I just grabbed it because it looked delicious

So that was total 14 different cuts of beef
Compared to the other restaurants, the meat has a very rich and deep flavor
Also, they were tender and juicy as well
Please come visit here in Gyeongju for its popular tourist spots and great restaurants!
Thank you!
